articles.title: TSX Starts the Week Strong
articles.expand: The S P/TSX Composite Index advanced 1.8% to close at 31,884 on Monday, as easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East triggered a sharp rotation back into risk assets. The reversal came on the heels of President Trump’s announcement of a five-day pause on planned strikes against Iranian energy infrastructure, a decision that promptly deflated ...

articles.title: Dollar Declines and Gold Plunges in Hopes Iran War Will Soon End
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) fell to a 1.5-week low on Monday and finished down by -0.65%. The dollar gave up overnight gains and turned lower as stocks rallied sharply after President Trump postponed attacks against Iranian energy infrastructure and power plants for five days following the start of talks...

articles.title: Economic and event calendar in Asia Tuesday, March 24, 2026 0 Japan inflation data
articles.expand: Japan’s national inflation data for February is due later today, with the release expected to mirror the softer tone seen in the Tokyo CPI figures earlier in the month. The Tokyo data showed underlying price pressures moderating, with core inflation slipping back below the Bank of Japan’s 2% target. This has reinforced the near-term case for mainta...

articles.title: Fed’s Daly flags two economic paths as Middle East risks cloud policy outlook
articles.expand: Daly signalled the Fed is navigating a highly uncertain outlook driven by geopolitical risks, with policy likely to remain flexible rather than guided by a fixed path. Summary: Daly outlines two scenarios tied to Middle East conflict trajectory Quick resolution → energy spike seen as temporary, policy can look through it Prolonged conflict → risks ...

articles.title: RBNZ’s Breman signals rate hike risk if energy shock drives persistent inflation
articles.expand: RBNZ signalled it will look through temporary energy-driven inflation but stands ready to hike rates if persistent price pressures threaten inflation expectations. Summary: RBNZ flags two-path outlook tied to Middle East-driven energy shock Short-lived spike → policymakers to look through petrol-driven inflation Prolonged disruption → risk of highe...

articles.title: Ominous: Thousands of US Marines are slated to arrive in the Middle East on Friday
articles.expand: In the lead-up to the war in Iran, there were all kinds of conflicting signals about what was coming. It was confusing and much of it was designed to misdirect. Evidently it worked as Iran's leaders met to discuss negotiations and the US/Israel used that to kill them. When I look back at the signs, the giveaway that a war was coming was the relentl...

articles.title: Oil plunges as Iran tensions ease. Crude oil futures settle at $88.13
articles.expand: Crude oil futures are settling sharply lower, down -$10.10 (-10.28%) to $88.13, after trading in a wide range between a high of $101.67 and a low of $84.37. The catalyst for the selloff came after President Trump’s Truth Social post, which signaled productive talks between the U.S. and Iran and a five-day postponement of planned strikes on Iranian ...

articles.title: AUD/USD steadies as Trump delays Iran strikes, risk sentiment improves
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades little changed on Monday after recovering from earlier losses, following US President Donald Trump's decision to delay planned strikes on Iran’s energy infrastructure, easing immediate geopolitical tensions and improving overall risk appetite.

articles.title: Markets tentatively embrace Trump's claims that a peace deal is likely
articles.expand: Do you trust Trump? That's the question that every market participant is asking today. The US President said several times today that the White House has had discussions with Iran about ending strikes and opening up the Strait of Hormuz. The problem is that Iran has denied every claim. There are possible reason for the mess of information including...

articles.title: USD/CHF retreats as easing Middle East tensions weigh on US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/CHF trades lower around 0.7870 on Monday, down 0.12% at the time of writing, as the US Dollar (USD) weakens following a temporary easing of geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: Global liquidity squeeze, HSI sharply lower on Trump ultimatum & the week ahead
articles.expand: We join OANDA Senior Market Analyst Kelvin Wong and podcast host Jonny Hart in today’s episode, recapping today’s Asia session after Trump’s ultimatum to Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz. Otherwise, we also look ahead to this week’s trading, with particular focus on upcoming PMI and inflation data.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs raises its US recession probability
articles.expand: The Wall Street Journal is reporting that rising energy prices tied to Middle East tensions are beginning to reshape the economic outlook, with Goldman Sachs warning that higher oil and gas costs, tighter financial conditions, and fading fiscal support are increasing downside risks for growth while lifting recession probabilities. Recession risk ri...

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Further consolidation should not be ruled out
articles.expand: For now, the near-term outlook for the Australian Dollar (AUD) remains constructive, underpinned by persistently high inflation in Australia and the RBA’s hawkish bias. This backdrop is likely to support further upside in AUD/USD while also offering a cushion against periodic pullbacks.

articles.title: NZD/USD edges higher as US Dollar weakens on geopolitical developments
articles.expand: NZD/USD trades around 0.5850 on Monday at the time of writing, up 0.24% on the day, benefiting from a broader pullback in the US Dollar (USD) amid a relative easing of geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: USDCHF breaks the 200 hour MA and runs to swing area support.
articles.expand: The USDCHF has been respecting its key technical levels, with the 200-hour moving average playing a central role in recent price action. On Friday—and again during the Asian-Pacific session today—the pair found support against the 200-hour moving average (currently at 0.7872). Buyers leaned against that level, helping to stabilize the downside and ...

articles.title: Eurozone: Firmer growth and eventual ECB hikes – BNP Paribas
articles.expand: BNP Paribas projects Eurozone growth at 1.6% in 2026, supported by German fiscal measures, higher military and AI-related investment, and a resilient labour market. Inflation is expected to stay below 2% in 2026 before gradually accelerating in 2027.

articles.title: USD/JPY: Energy shock and flows point higher – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG analysts see recent Yen weakness as fundamentally justified and expect further downside for the Japanese Yen if the Middle East crisis persists.

articles.title: ECB: Early insurance hike risks and data focus – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists Fabien Bossy, Michel Martinez, Anatoli Annenkov and Sam Cartwright argue that the ECB is edging toward discussing early insurance rate hikes after the recent energy shock.

articles.title: USDCAD sets the high from last week setting the ceiling and 200 hour MA the floor.
articles.expand: The USDCAD edged to a marginal new high for the year—stretching just a pip beyond the January 23 peak to 1.3755 (vs. 1.3754)—before quickly running into resistance. From there, price action reversed sharply following the Trump Truth Social headline, triggering a swift move lower. That downside momentum carried the pair into a key confluence zone, i...

articles.title: USD/JPY falls sharply as geopolitical easing weighs on US Dollar
articles.expand: USD/JPY falls by 0.40% on Monday and trades around 158.60 at the time of writing, after dropping from the 159.60 area to an intraday low near 158.25, as the US Dollar (USD) weakens following an unexpected geopolitical development.

articles.title: BoC: Rates seen on extended hold – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists Robert Both and Emma Lawrence expect the Bank of Canada (BoC) to keep the overnight rate at 2.25% throughout 2026, despite markets pricing significant tightening.

articles.title: GBP/USD: BoE repricing challenges rate cut view – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC highlights that the Bank of England’s (BoE) latest communication has triggered aggressive market repricing, with almost 85 bps of hikes now priced for 2026. This makes the bank’s previously expected BoE cut in 3Q26 less certain, with an extended hold more plausible.

articles.expand: EUR/USD sigue bajo presión, con soportes y resistencias vigiladas por inversores. Factores como la guerra en Irán y el petróleo influyen en la dinámica reciente del par.

articles.title: Panic at pumps, energy bill pain and surging food costs: Starmer holds crisis talks on potential Iran war fallout TODAY as he vows to use 'every lever' to help families... but can he even afford a bailout?
articles.expand: Keir Starmer will gather senior ministers and the Bank of England governor in Whitehall amid fears of panic at pumps, an inflation timebomb for energy and food bills, and interest rate rises.

articles.title: Nigeria’s Oil Earnings Drop to $31.5bn Amid Gas Boom
articles.expand: Nigeria recorded $31.54 billion in crude oil exports in 2025, down from the previous year despite higher production, according to data from the Central Bank of Nigeria. With oil revenues falling from $36.85 billion in 2024, the most recent balance of payments report underlines continuing obstacles in the main source of income for the [...]

articles.title: ‘War has costs’: Iran defends $2 million transit fee for ships crossing Hormuz
articles.expand: Iran starts charging $2M per ship in the Strait of Hormuz. Move defies Trump’s 48-hour deadline. Brent crude hits $120 as shipping insurance premiums skyrocket.

Hopes for an offramp to host...

articles.title: Global shares decline as hopes dim for resolution in Iran after Trump's latest comments
articles.expand: Global shares have slipped across the board, as oil prices continued to climb after President Donald Trump’s latest comments dashed hopes for an early end to the war in Iran

articles.title: UK 10-year gilt yields rise to highest since 2008 as markets price in 4 BoE hikes
articles.expand: British 10-year ​government bond yields ​rose on Monday to ​their highest since July 2008 at 5.065%, up 6 basis points ‌on the ⁠day, ⁠as markets priced in four ​Bank of England interest rate rises ​for this year.

articles.title: Goldman Sachs now sees ECB delivering a rate hike in April meeting
articles.expand: Goldman Sachs is joining in on the bandwagon in revising their call ahead of the ECB meeting next month. They had previously forecast the central bank to keep interest rates steady throughout this year. So, the change mirrors that of JP Morgan and Barclays from last week as both also expect the ECB to move in April. The firm cites inflation risks d...

articles.title: Japan weighs cutting inflation-linked bond buybacks as demand rises
articles.expand: Japan’s potential reduction in inflation-linked bond buybacks reflects rising inflation expectations and stronger demand, signalling a tentative shift away from its deflationary past. Summary: Japan considering reducing buybacks of inflation-linked bonds Move driven by rising investor demand amid higher inflation expectations Break-even inflation r...

articles.title: EUR/USD Weekly Forecast: Iran war to keep shaping the market’s sentiment
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair bounced back in the last few days, settling for the week around 1.1530. The Iran war and central banks’ monetary policy announcements took centre stage, yet none was enough to impress speculative interest.

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Pound tests 200-day SMA as hawkish BoE fuels recovery
articles.expand: The Pound Sterling (GBP) staged a late rebound from near three-month lows against the US Dollar (USD), testing the critical 1.3440 supply zone.

articles.title: Asset Purchase Facility: Gilt Sales – Market Notice 20 March 2026
articles.expand: This Market Notice sets out the schedule for sales in Q2 2026 of gilts held in the Asset Purchase Facility (APF) for monetary policy purposes.

articles.title: USD strength vs Euro vulnerability: What’s next for the EUR/USD pair?
articles.expand: EUR/USD tests key level (1.1578) as the ECB's hawkish stance battles downside risks from Middle East conflict and spiking energy costs. The article analyzes the "April Hike" wildcard, NFP volatility, and critical technical levels. Outlook is split between technical recovery and fundamental risks.

The pound responded positively to the Bank of England’s decision to keep interest rates unchanged, with market attention focused on the regulator’s guidance on how the Iran conflict might influence future policy. articles.title: BOE Announced “Hawkish Hold” As Inflation Risks Rose on US-Iran War
articles.expand: The BOE voted unanimously to keep Bank Rate on hold at 3.75% at its March 2026 meeting thanks to a sharp spike in global energy prices triggered by conflict in the Middle East.
